chunk_summary: This section outlines protocols for constipation disimpaction and maintenance in children with neurodisability, emphasizing nutritional management and evidence-based guidelines from IAP, ESPGHAN, and NASPGHAN.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of neurodisability in feeding challenges and constipation management.
Learn evidence-based disimpaction protocols for children with neurodisability.
Explore nutritional strategies recommended by ESPGHAN for children with neurological impairments.
Identify key guidelines for functional constipation treatment in pediatric populations.
key_facts: Constipation affects up to 30% of children with neurodisability, requiring tailored interventions.
Disimpaction protocols often involve polyethylene glycol (PEG) at doses of 1-1.5 g/kg/day for 3-6 days.
Maintenance therapy includes dietary fiber intake of 0.5 g/kg/day and adequate hydration.
ESPGHAN recommends individualized nutritional plans for children with neurological impairments to prevent constipation.
NASPGHAN guidelines emphasize early intervention to prevent chronic constipation complications.
reference_quote: ESPGHAN recommends individualized nutritional management for children with neurological impairments to address feeding challenges and prevent constipation.
document_key_topics: Nutritional management in children with neurological impairments
Evidence-based guidelines for pediatric constipation treatment
